trajectory_summary: The call opens on a warm, cooperative lead and Owen books the appointment — a functional outcome. But the energy shape is stagnant: Owen never anchors the bill pain Chris volunteered ('a doozy'), never creates curiosity, and drifts into cat and football small talk while the scheduler loads. The booking is real but thin — no emotional investment was built that would make Chris show up.
outcome_dispute: v1 outcome tag is unknown. Based on the transcript, the call ends with a booked appointment (March 18, 2PM). The booking is real but structurally fragile: no bill-pain anchor, no curiosity creation, no 'is that fair' verbal handshake, no computer/no-driving confirmation, and no text follow-up promise made. The appointment should be tagged 'booked — fragile' rather than a clean sit.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Bill-pain anchor: capture + flip, not capture + drop
primary_coaching_focus.quote: The one last month was a doozy.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Chris handed Owen the emotional core of the pitch unprompted and Owen let it pass. This is the universal floor gap per coaching_philosophy.bill_anchoring — every rep in the corpus captures the bill and none flip it. On this call the signal was especially loud ('a doozy') and the miss is clean. Fixing this one move changes the quality of every appointment Owen books.

key_moments.quote: The one last month was a doozy.
key_moments.stewart_read: Chris volunteers bill pain without being asked — the highest-value moment in the call. Owen's response ('I can't wait to see it') is pleasant but drops the emotional signal entirely. The schema requires capture + flip: acknowledge the pain, then redirect it against the utility. Owen captured nothing and flipped nothing. Reactive read holds: this is a missed anchor, not a strategic deferral — there is no later moment in the call where Owen returns to the bill pain.

key_moments.quote: By the way, your house is perfect. Like, no trees is is a blessing.
key_moments.stewart_read: Owen correctly identifies the sun-exposure advantage and names it positively. This is a partial curiosity-creation move — he is pointing at a real qualifier that makes Chris's home a strong candidate. The positive signal is surfaced but not extended: Owen does not connect the sun exposure to Chris's bill pain or to what the design will show. The moment lands as a compliment rather than as a hook.

Chris reads his consumption number aloud — exactly what the verbal read-back pivot in bill_collection.alternative_paths is designed to capture. Owen does not acknowledge or record the number. Instead, at 06:26 he asks again whether the graph is on the backside of the bill. The data was delivered; Owen missed it. This is a reactive failure, not a strategic deferral.

key_moments.quote: I've got new windows coming Monday.
key_moments.stewart_read: Chris mentions new windows arriving Monday — a home-improvement cycle signal. A customer actively investing in energy efficiency (new roof last year, new windows this week) is a higher-intent solar prospect than baseline. This stacking pattern is not yet formalized in the schema (TBD gap), but coaching_philosophy.curiosity_over_features is the closest applicable principle: the rep's job is to make Chris curious about his specific numbers, and a customer already spending on efficiency upgrades is primed for that curiosity. Owen's response ('Yeah. It's not — that's just not let me try again') is a text-send attempt, not an acknowledgment of the signal.
